author: maintainer-agent

patch.value: Current todos export writes full front matter but todos import only hydrates a subset of ItemFrontMatter. This silently drops definition_of_ready, planning metadata, blocked metadata, reviewer/risk/confidence/release, and issue-specific fields on re-import. Update docs first then implement import normalization and regression coverage so round-trips are lossless for canonical fields.
Restore full todos import metadata parity
Preserve current ItemFrontMatter optional metadata when importing todos markdown so import/export round-trips do not silently drop planning, workflow, and issue fields.

Todos import preserves canonical optional front-matter fields currently supported by ItemFrontMatter including planning workflow and issue metadata; PRD and README describe the parity guarantee; targeted and full sandboxed regressions pass with 100% coverage preserved.
Gap reproduced by code inspection against ItemFrontMatter and no active duplicate item exists.
Prevents silent metadata loss in extension workflows.
Improves migration safety and cross-tool compatibility for real project tracking.
Todos markdown round-trips preserve canonical pm metadata.
Milestone 5 still documents todos hardening as partial and current import behavior contradicts that intent.
Users importing todos markdown can currently lose planning and issue metadata without warning.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Why this exists todos import currently drops many canonical front matter fields even though export writes them.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Plan update docs first then hydrate missing fields in todos import and add round trip regression coverage.

message: Create todos import parity follow-up from release-readiness gap scan | explicit_unset=learnings,assignee,reviewer,blocked_by,blocked_reason,unblock_note,reporter,severity,environment,repro_steps,resolution,expected_result,actual_result,affected_version,fixed_version

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Planned changeset: update PRD and README to state todos import preserves canonical optional ItemFrontMatter fields during round-trip import-export, then hydrate missing import mappings in src/extensions/builtins/todos/import-export.ts and add regression coverage in tests/unit/todos-extension.spec.ts before running pm test and pm test-all sweeps.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Implementation update: PRD and README now state todos import preserves canonical optional ItemFrontMatter metadata when present. src/extensions/builtins/todos/import-export.ts now hydrates the missing planning workflow and issue metadata fields with deterministic enum and boolean normalization, and tests/unit/todos-extension.spec.ts adds a focused regression fixture covering those fields.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Coverage follow-up: added regression alias coverage for todos import so string false and numeric 1/0 inputs exercise the new boolean normalization branches before rerunning the item-linked coverage gate.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Coverage follow-up: added enum sanitization coverage for numeric order, empty risk, invalid severity, and direct boolean regression passthrough so the new todos import helpers meet the 100% coverage gate.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Coverage follow-up: added normalization edge coverage for order=not-an-integer, regression="0", and unsupported regression=2 so the remaining todos import helper branches are exercised without broadening runtime behavior.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Coverage follow-up: added unsupported regression string coverage so todos import boolean normalization now exercises the remaining string fallthrough branch before final validation.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Validation note: concurrent pm test-all sweeps contend on shared coverage artifacts in this repo, so release validation is being rerun sequentially after one parallel in_progress sweep showed a false-negative coverage drop despite standalone pm test passing at 100%.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Evidence: pnpm build passed on current changes. pm test pm-ecbn --run --timeout 7200 passed both linked commands after targeted coverage follow-ups; node scripts/run-tests.mjs coverage reports 100% statements branches functions and lines, and node scripts/run-tests.mjs test -- tests/unit/todos-extension.spec.ts passed 20/20. Sequential regression sweeps then passed: pm test-all --status in_progress --timeout 7200 => items=1 linked_tests=2 passed=2 failed=0 skipped=0; pm test-all --status closed --timeout 7200 => items=150 linked_tests=382 passed=68 failed=0 skipped=314. Note: parallel test-all sweeps produced a false-negative coverage race on shared coverage artifacts, so final evidence uses the sequential rerun.

author: maintainer-agent

patch.value: Todos import now preserves canonical optional metadata and the full sequential regression sweep is green.

message: Close: todos import metadata parity restored with 100% coverage and sequential pm test-all evidence
